schema_version: v0 — migrates when Q6 (intention envelope) lands
owner: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (CC) — CC gates writes; Akien-ratified (signature gate, 2026-07-23 design conversation: name is `cairn` in full — 'i thought we'd already decided cairn as the name', 'let's not do a shortening at this time. cairn is not very long.'; 'sorted.').
intention: The user-facing command runner: a `bin/` at the repo root (`~/dev/src/cairn/bin/`) with a single dispatcher `cairn`, so Akien invokes any Cairn facility by `cairn <name> <args...>` instead of memorizing paths. v0 is UU's pattern exactly — `cairn <name> ...` execs `bin/cmd/<name> <args...>` — one thin file, no resolution smarts until a real need pulls them in. The NAME is `cairn` in full (no 2-letter shortening; measured free on PATH — `cc` is the C compiler and already means CC-the-mind, so it was doubly wrong). Any ergonomic shorthand is a PERSONAL shell alias in instance-space, never baked into the shared tree.
why: Founding intention — get Akien out of hand-wiring the system: a named front door for every facility beats a remembered path, and it is the seam every later `cairn <verb>` (compile, scan, intent, ...) hangs off. Putting the FULL name in git and leaving the cramped shorthand to a personal alias keeps the class/instance line honest: the durable shared thing is the self-documenting name (`cairn compile` reads in any ticket or doc); the fingers-preference 2-letter form is per-instance personal data (dotfiles, `~/.cairn`), which is exactly where an instance concern belongs. Minimalism: the dispatcher owns NO logic of the facilities it runs — it only resolves a name to a script and hands off (Law 6 — each facility owns itself).
traces_to: founding intention (get Akien out of running the system — a named route, not a memorized path)
Law 1 (the resolver is not spent re-typing settled paths — the route is compiled into a name)
Law 6 (the dispatcher owns nothing it runs — it resolves a name and hands off to that facility's own gate)
the roots table (class-space is shareable/git; the ergonomic shorthand is instance-space personal data, never in git — CLAUDE.md three-roots)
quarried metaphor: UU's `uu $1 $2 ...` script-prefix dispatcher (the pattern kept; the name is Cairn's own)
state: code-seam@v1: THINKME -> TICKETME -> [BUILDME] -> PROVEME -> LEARNME -> PROVED   (cursor at BUILDME: design wrapped + ratified this session; NO blocking deps; buildable now. A host-seam EDGE rides along — making `cairn` callable from anywhere needs `bin/` on PATH, which lives where git cannot see it (~/.bashrc / a profile.d drop-in); that edge carries a replayable `apply` (prepend `<repo>/bin` to PATH) + a re-runnable `verify` (`command -v cairn` resolves into the repo), and its seal expires when the host drifts. The dispatcher SCRIPT is the code-seam and is git-tracked; the PATH wiring is the host-seam it depends on.)
gates: proof gate (tester) — a proof a hollow build could not pass: a `bin/cmd/<name>` stub is invoked by `cairn <name> a b c` with the args passed through verbatim and its exit code propagated; an UNKNOWN name fails LOUD (non-zero, a legible 'no such command', CP1 — no silent no-op); the dispatcher adds no output of its own on the success path (it is a pass-through, not a wrapper). VALIDATION persisted beside-code via cairn/tester/validation_store.persist_validation (validations/<stem>.json beside the proof) and grep back. Durability gate: proven, persisted, committed (git).
falsifier: `cairn <name> <args>` runs `bin/cmd/<name>` with args verbatim and exit code intact, and an unknown name fails loud (never a silent no-op). Wrong-shape signals: (1) the dispatcher grows knowledge of WHAT its subcommands do (special-casing a facility instead of resolving-and-handing-off) — it has stopped being a dumb runner and started owning logic that belongs to the facility (Law 6); (2) the short 2-letter name reappears baked into the git tree (an instance concern crossing into class-space); (3) a second dispatcher grows elsewhere (the route stopped being one named front door).
horizon: first real subcommand — when `cairn compile` (intentions-model-compiler) runs THROUGH this dispatcher end to end, plus at least one more verb, re-check: is the flat `bin/cmd/<name>` passthrough still enough, or does a real facility need argument-routing / a subcommand tree (grow the resolution against that need, not before)? And confirm the PATH host-seam's verify still seals on a fresh shell.
waits_on: nothing — buildable now. It is the front door the intentions-model-compiler's `cairn compile` verb (and later `cairn scan`, `cairn intent`, ...) hang off; those facilities are separate nodes that register a `bin/cmd/<name>` against this dispatcher.
provenance: cast 2026-07-23 via /sorted, at the close of a design conversation that also cast intentions-model-compiler. Born from an /intent that bundled three linked ideas — a repo-scanner device, an intentions-folder updater, and a user-facing `bin/` command runner with a short prefix. The prefix question (cc/cs/cr/ccs) resolved by MEASURING PATH conflicts (only `cc` taken — the C compiler) and by placing the ergonomic shorthand on the correct side of the class/instance line: full `cairn` in git, personal alias in dotfiles. Akien: 'let's not do a shortening at this time. cairn is not very long. we'll see how it goes.'
